active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution for global optimization of circle centers
active_paradigms.description: Treat circle center positions (52 variables for 26 circles) as optimization variables. Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution with bounds [0,1] for each coordinate. The objective function computes maximum feasible radii given current positions (using the existing compute_max_radii logic) and returns negative sum (since DE minimizes). Add penalty terms for constraint violations. Use strategy='best1bin' with reasonable population size. This global optimizer can escape local optima that trap the current fixed-pattern approach.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize sum of radii by finding globally optimal circle positions
active_paradigms.cautions: Use bounds=(0,1) for all 52 variables, set maxiter appropriately for timeout, polish=True for final refinement, use seed for reproducibility

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.distance.cdist for efficient constraint computation with iterative position refinement
active_paradigms.description: Build a greedy incremental placement algorithm: place circles one by one, using scipy.spatial.distance.cdist to efficiently compute all pairwise distances. For each new circle, find the position that maximizes its potential radius by considering distances to all existing circles and walls. Use a grid-based search for position candidates, then refine with golden section search along promising directions. This exploits the O(n²) distance computation efficiently while building a better arrangement.
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Position each circle optimally relative to already-placed circles
active_paradigms.cautions: Order of placement matters significantly - try largest-first or corner-first orderings, use fine enough grid for initial search

active_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with trust-constr for constrained nonlinear optimization of positions and radii jointly
active_paradigms.description: Formulate as constrained optimization: maximize sum(radii) subject to non-overlap constraints (||c_i - c_j|| >= r_i + r_j) and boundary constraints (r_i <= x_i, r_i <= 1-x_i, etc.). Use scipy.optimize.minimize with method='trust-constr' which handles nonlinear constraints well. Define constraints using NonlinearConstraint with appropriate Jacobian. Start from the current hexagonal pattern positions as initial guess. Jointly optimize all 78 variables (52 centers + 26 radii).
active_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Jointly optimize positions and radii under geometric constraints
active_paradigms.cautions: Constraint Jacobians improve convergence significantly, use sparse Jacobian structure, warm start from good initial positions, set appropriate tolerance

tried_paradigms.idea: Use numpy.linalg.lstsq to solve the system of equations where circles touch their neighbors and walls optimally
tried_paradigms.description: Model the packing as a system where each circle touches exactly 3 neighbors or walls (stable configuration). For n circles, set up equations: for circle i touching circle j, |c_i - c_j| = r_i + r_j. Linearize by assuming contact directions and solve for positions and radii simultaneously. Use least squares when the system is overdetermined. Start from hexagonal pattern, identify which contacts should be active, solve the linearized system, then iterate with corrected contact directions. This directly computes optimal positions rather than searching.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Circle positions and radii by solving the geometric contact equations
tried_paradigms.cautions: Must handle the nonlinearity of distance constraints through iteration. The contact graph must be valid (achievable packing). Use multiple contact graph configurations based on different row patterns.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi for position optimization via centroid shifting
tried_paradigms.description: Start with initial hexagonal positions, compute Voronoi diagram to identify neighbors and free space. Iteratively shift each circle center toward the centroid of its Voronoi cell (clipped to unit square), which naturally moves circles away from neighbors and walls. This creates more room for radius expansion. Use 20-30 iterations of Voronoi computation and centroid shifting, then apply the existing radius expansion. This is pure geometric reasoning without optimization functions.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize sum_radii by creating better initial positions that allow larger radius expansion
tried_paradigms.cautions: Clip Voronoi vertices to [0,1] range. Handle boundary cells carefully - their Voronoi regions extend outside the square. Use scipy.spatial.Voronoi directly, not through optimization.

tried_paradigms.idea: Systematic pattern enumeration with numpy to find optimal row arrangement
tried_paradigms.description: The current 5-4-5-4-5-3 pattern may not be optimal. Test multiple hexagonal patterns systematically: 4-5-4-5-4-4, 6-5-5-4-3+3, 5-5-5-5-3-3, 6-4-6-4-4-2, etc. For each pattern, compute optimal spacing analytically: for n_rows with alternating counts, the ideal spacing maximizes minimum distance to walls. Use numpy to compute radius sums for each pattern and select the best. This explores the discrete pattern space without optimization.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Find the hexagonal pattern arrangement that maximizes sum_radii
tried_paradigms.cautions: Ensure each pattern sums to exactly 26 circles. Account for different vertical spacing needs based on row count. Use numpy for efficient computation across patterns.

tried_paradigms.idea: Corner-first strategic placement with radius-aware positioning
tried_paradigms.description: Place circles strategically: 4 large circles in corners (constrained by 2 walls), then circles along edges (constrained by 1 wall), then fill interior. For corner circles, position at (r,r), (1-r,r), (r,1-r), (1-r,1-r) and expand maximally first. Then place edge circles with optimal spacing along each edge. Finally place interior circles in hexagonal pattern. This exploits the geometric insight that corner/edge positions have different constraints and should be treated differently.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize sum_radii by prioritizing constrained positions and using radius-aware placement
tried_paradigms.cautions: Corner circles compete for space along edges. Must carefully balance corner radius vs edge circle sizes. Interior placement must avoid all placed circles. Use pure geometric construction with numpy.

tried_paradigms.description: Treat circles as particles that repel each other while staying in bounds. Use scipy.integrate.odeint to simulate forces: circles repel each other and walls, then compute radii from final positions. This is a physics-based approach fundamentally different from optimization.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize radii sum by letting circles naturally find optimal positions through physics simulation
tried_paradigms.cautions: Use soft boundaries, not hard constraints. Use scipy.integrate.odeint for integration.

tried_paradigms.description: Start with current hexagonal pattern. Apply random perturbations to positions, accept if score improves. Use Metropolis acceptance criteria with decreasing temperature. This escapes local minima and explores the configuration space globally.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize sum of radii by accepting position perturbations that improve the total radius sum
tried_paradigms.cautions: Use geometric cooling schedule, run multiple restarts. Keep track of best solution found.

tried_paradigms.idea: Optimal circle packing patterns for n=26
tried_paradigms.description: Use known optimal patterns for n=26. Research shows that for specific n, optimal packings often follow specific patterns. Use scipy.spatial.Delaunay to analyze neighbor distances and optimize circle positions to maximize minimum distance to neighbors and walls.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Minimize the maximum ratio of actual distance to desired distance for all circle pairs
tried_paradigms.cautions: This is not an optimization problem but a pattern-matching approach. Use Delaunay triangulation to find neighbors, then iterate positions to maximize packing efficiency.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.minimize with SLSQP to jointly optimize all 78 variables (26 center positions and 26 radii) with explicit nonlinear constraints for non-overlap and boundary conditions.
tried_paradigms.description: Formulate the circle packing as a constrained nonlinear optimization problem. Create variables array [x1,y1,r1, x2,y2,r2, ..., x26,y26,r26] with initial positions from the current hexagonal pattern. Define objective function as negative sum of radii (to maximize sum). Create constraint functions: (1) For each circle i: ri <= xi, ri <= yi, ri <= 1-xi, ri <= 1-yi (boundary constraints), (2) For each pair i<j: (xi-xj)^2 + (yi-yj)^2 >= (ri+rj)^2 (non-overlap). Use SLSQP method which handles nonlinear constraints. Start with multiple initial configurations (hexagonal, grid, random perturbations) to find global optimum.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Maximize sum of all 26 radii subject to non-overlap and boundary constraints
tried_paradigms.cautions: Non-overlap constraints are non-convex (quadratic), so use multiple random restarts. Scale variables appropriately. Use tolerance settings to ensure valid solutions. May need 10-20 restarts from different initial configurations to find good local optima.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.spatial.distance.pdist and scipy.spatial.distance.squareform to compute pairwise distances, then implement a greedy expansion algorithm with priority queue based on marginal radius gain per unit space consumed.
tried_paradigms.description: Start with small equal-radius circles at optimized positions. Compute all pairwise distances using pdist/squareform for O(n^2) distance matrix. For each circle, calculate its expansion potential: how much radius can be added before hitting a wall or another circle. Use a priority queue where priority = expansion_potential * position_weight, where position_weight favors corners and edges (less constrained). Iteratively expand the highest-priority circle by a small amount, update distance matrix, recalculate potentials, and repeat until no expansion is possible. This greedy approach with smart prioritization can find better local optima than uniform expansion.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Greedy expansion order and position weights to maximize final sum of radii
tried_paradigms.cautions: Greedy may get stuck in local optima. Try different priority functions (linear, quadratic expansion potential). Position weights should reflect constraint tightness. Use small expansion steps (0.001) for precision.

tried_paradigms.idea: Use scipy.optimize.differential_evolution as a global optimizer to find optimal circle positions and radii, treating it as a bound-constrained problem with penalty function for constraint violations.
tried_paradigms.description: Differential evolution is a stochastic global optimizer well-suited for non-convex problems. Encode solution as 78-dimensional vector (26 centers + 26 radii). Define bounds: centers in [0,1], radii in [0, 0.5]. Create objective function that returns negative sum of radii plus large penalty for constraint violations: penalty = sum of max(0, ri+rj - dij) for all overlapping pairs + sum of boundary violations. Differential evolution will explore the search space globally and can escape local optima that trap gradient-based methods. Use population size of 15-20, mutation and recombination parameters around 0.5-0.7. Run for sufficient generations (100-200) to converge.
tried_paradigms.what_to_optimize: Global search for optimal configuration maximizing sum of radii while satisfying constraints
tried_paradigms.cautions: Penalty function must be carefully tuned - too small and constraints are violated, too large and search becomes difficult. Use adaptive penalty scaling. May need longer runtime than gradient methods but finds better global solutions. Ensure enough generations for convergence.
